title: Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president expressed concern about the consequences of the war in the Middle East on the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow. Pressured by both the Americans and the Russians to give up the Donbas, he defended the region's strategic importance and warned Europeans of the urgent need to lift the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan approved in 2025.

title: France bids farewell to former prime minister Lionel Jospin in national ceremony

Sarkozy minister's defense unsettled by memo in Libyan campaign funding appeal
subhead: A document pledging Libyan funding for Nicolas Sarkozy's 2007 presidential campaign caught former minister Brice Hortefeux off guard during the appeal hearing. The memo's authenticity has been greatly debated during the investigation and first trial.

title: South Africa says disinvited from G7 summit after US pressured France
subhead: 'We are told that the Americans threatened to boycott the G7 if South Africa was invited,' reported the presidential spokesperson on Thursday.

title: Night train service resumes between Paris and Berlin
subhead: The Belgian-Dutch cooperative European Sleeper is reviving the symbolic rail link between the French and German capitals that was discontinued by SNCF in December 2025.

title: The sensuality of Henri Rousseau's paintings at the Musée de l'Orangerie
subhead: The exhibition 'Henri Rousseau: A Painter's Ambition' offers a stimulating reinterpretation of the artist's work, highlighting his rigor and discipline, far removed from the cliché of the 'naïve' or 'Sunday painter.'

subhead: Less than three months before the 2026 World Cup, the former French maestro is the clear frontrunner to succeed France's football squad head coach Didier Deschamps, who will step down at the end of the competition.

title: Emmanuel Grégoire's long run to Paris City Hall
subhead: The next mayor of Paris, who will be officially elected by the city council on Sunday, will now step into the spotlight after years in the shadow of his political mentors.

title: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
subhead: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.
